ControlDescription: LLM applications may inadvertently reveal confidential data through responses
VulnerableState: RAG retrieves and summarizes any document regardless of user clearance — payroll and credentials exposed to Public-clearance user
RemediatedState: Pre-retrieval clearance filter blocks sensitive documents before LLM context is built — LLM never sees unauthorized content

ControlDescription: Attackers manipulate LLM prompts to override instructions or extract data
VulnerableState: No input validation — user query sent directly to retrieval and LLM without sanitization
RemediatedState: System prompt separation and context isolation — user input never mixed with system instructions

ControlDescription: LLM outputs passed downstream without validation enabling data leakage
VulnerableState: LLM response returned directly with no output filtering — sensitive data printed verbatim
RemediatedState: Redaction applied to boundary-level documents before LLM context — amounts and IDs masked

ControlDescription: Organizational policies define roles responsibilities and accountability for AI risk
VulnerableState: No access policy enforced — all users treated as equally trusted regardless of role
RemediatedState: Sensitivity labels and clearance levels defined and enforced — every access decision tied to user role

ControlDescription: Identify and document risks associated with AI system context and use
VulnerableState: Oversharing risk not identified or mitigated — system designed with no data classification awareness
RemediatedState: Risk identified and mapped: LLM06 oversharing reproduced in baseline then mitigated in remediated state

ControlDescription: Monitor AI system behavior and measure risk indicators over time
VulnerableState: No logging — zero visibility into what documents were retrieved or what the LLM responded
RemediatedState: Full audit log captures timestamp / user / clearance / query / decision / blocked docs / LLM response

ControlDescription: Implement controls to treat identified AI risks to acceptable levels
VulnerableState: No controls implemented — vulnerable by design
RemediatedState: Three controls layered: pre-retrieval filter + boundary redaction + hard refusal when no cleared docs available

ControlDescription: Adversary queries an LLM to extract sensitive training or retrieval data
VulnerableState: External contractor (Dave) retrieves Highly Confidential payroll and credential documents via semantic query
RemediatedState: Pre-retrieval filter prevents any document above user clearance from entering LLM context — attack surface eliminated

ControlDescription: Adversary manipulates retrieval to surface unauthorized documents through crafted queries
VulnerableState: No query sanitization — adversary can craft queries to semantically target sensitive documents
RemediatedState: Clearance check applied post-retrieval regardless of query content — even targeted queries blocked if user lacks clearance

ControlDescription: Organizations shall identify and assess risks associated with AI systems throughout lifecycle
VulnerableState: No risk assessment performed — system deployed without considering data sensitivity or user trust levels
RemediatedState: Risks identified at design time: sensitivity labels assigned to all documents clearance levels assigned to all users

ControlDescription: Organizations shall implement controls to treat identified AI risks
VulnerableState: No risk treatment — vulnerable baseline represents untreated risk
RemediatedState: Three-layer control implemented: access filter + redaction + refusal. Audit log provides evidence of control operation

ControlDescription: Organizations shall implement logging to support accountability and traceability of AI decisions
VulnerableState: No logging in vulnerable system — no audit trail of queries responses or access decisions
RemediatedState: Every query logged with full context: user clearance query decision blocked/cleared docs and LLM response
